title: Prohibited artificial intelligence practices
summary: Bans AI systems that use subliminal techniques, exploit vulnerabilities, enable social scoring, or use real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces

title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
summary: Defines criteria for classifying AI as high-risk based on use in safety components, critical infrastructure, and other high-impact areas

title: Amendments to Annex III
summary: Empowers the European Commission to update the list of high-risk AI use cases in Annex III via delegated acts

title: Compliance with requirements for high-risk AI systems
summary: High-risk AI systems must comply with the requirements set out in Chapter III Section 2, taking into account intended purpose

title: Risk management system
summary: Requires providers of high-risk AI to establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system throughout the AI system lifecycle

title: Data and data governance
summary: Sets requirements for training, validation, and testing data used in high-risk AI systems including data governance practices and bias prevention

title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
summary: High-risk AI systems must be designed to be sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret and use their outputs appropriately

title: Human oversight
summary: High-risk AI systems must enable effective human oversight by natural persons during use, including ability to override or stop the system

title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
summary: High-risk AI systems must achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity throughout their lifecycle

title: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
summary: Lists obligations for providers of high-risk AI systems including quality management, technical documentation, conformity assessment, and registration

title: Quality management system
summary: Providers of high-risk AI systems must implement a quality management system covering strategies, testing procedures, and post-market monitoring

title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
summary: Deployers of high-risk AI must follow provider instructions, assign human oversight, monitor operation, and suspend use if risks are identified

title: Fundamental rights impact assessment for high-risk AI systems
summary: Public sector bodies and certain private entities deploying high-risk AI must conduct a fundamental rights impact assessment before deployment

title: Responsibilities along the AI value chain
summary: Clarifies provider obligations when modifying AI systems and sets responsibilities for distributors and importers in the AI supply chain

title: Obligations of importers
summary: Importers must verify high-risk AI conformity assessment, ensure providers have technical documentation, and maintain records

title: Harmonised standards and common specifications
summary: AI systems conforming to harmonised standards or common specifications are presumed to comply with the relevant requirements of the Act

title: Common specifications
summary: When harmonised standards are absent or insufficient, the Commission may adopt common specifications through implementing acts

title: Conformity assessment
summary: Specifies conformity assessment procedures for high-risk AI, requiring third-party assessment for biometric identification and certain other uses

title: Registration
summary: Providers and deployers must register high-risk AI systems in the EU-wide database before placing on market or putting into service

title: Transparency obligations for providers and deployers of certain AI systems
summary: Requires disclosure when users interact with AI systems including chatbots, and mandates labelling of AI-generated synthetic content and deepfakes

title: Classification of GPAI models
summary: Defines classification rules for general-purpose AI models, with special obligations for those posing systemic risk above 10^25 FLOPs training compute

title: Obligations for providers of GPAI models
summary: GPAI model providers must maintain technical documentation, respect copyright, publish summaries of training data, and provide usage policies

title: Obligations for providers of GPAI models with systemic risk
summary: Providers of GPAI models with systemic risk must conduct adversarial testing, report serious incidents, ensure cybersecurity, and limit energy use

title: Penalties
summary: Sets administrative fines up to €35M or 7% of annual turnover for prohibited AI violations, €15M or 3% for other violations, €7.5M for misinformation

title: Penalties for Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies
summary: The European Data Protection Supervisor may impose fines on EU institutions and bodies for violations of the AI Act
